title: [FIRRTL] Restore subaccess from multibit_mux in aggregate preservation mode
body:
input:
```scala
circuit Foo:
  module Foo:
    input a: UInt<1>[4]
    input sel: UInt<2>
    output b: UInt<1>
    b <= a[sel]
```
`firtool -preserve-aggregate -preserve-public-types=false -imconstprop=false`

output is:
```sv
module Foo(	// foo.fir:2:10
  input  [3:0] a,
  input  [1:0] sel,
  output       b);

  wire [3:0] _T = {{a[2'h3]}, {a[2'h2]}, {a[2'h1]}, {a[2'h0]}};	// foo.fir:6:12
  assign b = _T[sel];	// foo.fir:2:10, :6:12
endmodule
```

We want to have:
```sv
 assign b = a[sel];
```

LowerTypes eliminates subaccess to make things simple in the down streams passes. However, in the aggregate preservation mode, we can use the original aggregate array like `assign b = a[sel]`.  Most of the passes assume that subaccess ops are already removed, so we should restore them just before LowerToHW (maybe during MergeConnections).
